title.en: What the team kept after you left
title.es: Lo que el equipo conservó después de que te fuiste

question.en: Tell me about a team or project you left in a better place. What specifically is better — in a way that would still be true even if every feature you personally built was removed?
question.es: Cuéntame sobre un equipo o proyecto que dejaste en un mejor lugar. ¿Qué específicamente está mejor — de una manera que seguiría siendo cierta incluso si se eliminara cada función que construiste personalmente?

must_include: a specific capability, process, or person's skill that the team now has — not the candidate's features, but something durable: a runbook, an architectural pattern, a testing discipline, a teammate who can now independently do something they couldn't before

what the candidate specifically did to create or transfer that capability — not 'I contributed to a positive culture' but the concrete mechanism: wrote and ran practice incident drills, gave ownership of a specific module, established a review process that the team continued using
at least one named person who is part of the stronger team — if no teammate is named, the story is about the candidate's output, not the team's strength

evidence that the improvement persists independent of the candidate — a teammate still using the process, a successor who relied on the documentation, a junior engineer who was promoted based on skills developed during the candidate's tenure
an honest assessment of what is NOT better — 'everything improved' is not credible; naming what the candidate did not change (and why) demonstrates calibration
must_avoid: output metrics as the legacy — 'our velocity doubled' or 'we shipped 3 major features' may reflect the candidate's contribution but are not durable team capabilities; they describe what was done, not what the team can now do
relationship capital as the legacy — 'I built strong relationships across the org' is personal capital that leaves with the candidate; it is not team-strengthening in the ST sense
vague culture claims — 'I fostered a culture of collaboration' without a named artifact or named person who carries that culture forward
features as legacy — if every named improvement is a feature the candidate built, this is an Excellence story, not a ST story; the distinction is between what was delivered and what the team now has
